prompts.user: Apply the same two-stage physical test to every case. Stage 1: use only the constitutive equation, not associations with material names, to determine whether the response increases, decreases, or remains unchanged as the control increases while all other quantities are fixed. For linear shear response, the response is shear strain gamma, the control is shear stress tau, and the relation is gamma = tau/G, with G fixed and positive. All stated coefficients are positive. Complete this relation step silently. In an elastic aluminum element, shear stress tau changes from 50 MPa to 5 MPa. Determine the numerical-change direction silently. Now compose the relation with the numerical change to determine the direction of shear strain gamma. Reply with exactly one lowercase word from this ordered set: higher, lower, unchanged.

For capillary rise at fixed tube radius, the response is capillary height h_c, the control is surface tension gamma_s, and the relation is h_c/gamma_s = C. All stated coefficients are positive. Complete this relation step silently. In a wetting liquid in a glass tube, surface tension gamma_s changes from 0.08 N m^-1 to 0.02 N m^-1. Determine the numerical-change direction silently. Now compose the relation with the numerical change to determine the direction of capillary height h_c. Reply with exactly one lowercase word from this ordered set: higher, lower, unchanged.

Stage 1: use only the constitutive equation, not associations with material names, to determine whether the response increases, decreases, or remains unchanged as the control increases while all other quantities are fixed. For steady creep accumulation, the response is creep strain epsilon_c, the control is exposure time t, and the relation is epsilon_c = eps_dot t, with eps_dot fixed and positive. All stated coefficients are positive. Complete this relation step silently. In a superalloy at fixed stress and temperature, exposure time t changes from 10 hours to 1000 hours. Determine the numerical-change direction silently. Now compose the relation with the numerical change to determine the direction of creep strain epsilon_c. Reply with exactly one lowercase word from this ordered set: higher, lower, unchanged.

Stage 1: use only the constitutive equation, not associations with material names, to determine whether the response increases, decreases, or remains unchanged as the control increases while all other quantities are fixed. For specific heat independent of sample mass, the response is specific heat c_p, the control is sample mass m_s, and the relation is c_p/m_s^0 = C. All stated coefficients are positive. Complete this relation step silently. In a homogeneous metal at fixed temperature, sample mass m_s changes from 1 g to 1 kg. Determine the numerical-change direction silently. Now compose the relation with the numerical change to determine the direction of specific heat c_p. Reply with exactly one lowercase word from this ordered set: unchanged, lower, higher.

For atomic mass independent of temperature, the response is atomic mass m_a, the control is temperature T, and the relation is m_a/T^0 = C. All stated coefficients are positive. Complete this relation step silently. In one isotope below ionization, temperature T changes from 100 K to 1000 K. Determine the numerical-change direction silently. Now compose the relation with the numerical change to determine the direction of atomic mass m_a. Reply with exactly one lowercase word from this ordered set: unchanged, lower, higher.
